briefing.headline: Rig up a Chatterbait — aggressive pre-spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Chatterbait is a strong choice for pre-spawn with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: Thunder Cricket in shallow grass even in upper 30s water. Use 3/8 oz (lighter than normal) with straight-tail blade minnow trailer for subtle action. Pull and stop, not steady retrieve. Get it down in old dead vegetation — it holds heat and bass are drawn to it. Tungsten model has smaller blade

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, riprap, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: Post-frontal cold pre-spawn — work the Thunder Cricket like a jig. Get it down in the grass, hop it, twitch it. Let it hit bottom or grass, then twitch. 'Sometimes you got to make them react.' Bi
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, riprap, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: For a spring pre-spawn event limited to three rods, picks a jerkbait/crankbait for deeper staging fish, a 1/2-oz jig for versatility, and a ChatterBait for stained shallow water.

KVD: In cold/post-frontal pre-spawn, don't just wind the Thunder Cricket. Work it like a jig — hop, twitch, pull-and-stop. Get it down in the grass and on the bottom. Bites are extremely subtle: the bait just gets heavy or the vibration stops. 'I ...

Palaniuk: 'The spot had a riprap bank with a steep drop, then a trough that came up onto a hard bottom ridge, tapered back off into another trough. The trough made a horseshoe. A 40-yard section where the fish wanted to live, with a 25-foot hole t...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal pre-spawn bass are aggressive and moving shallow. Expect fish in the 4-8ft range on secondary points and channel swings. Falling pressure pulls fish up from staging areas — target the first breakline off spawning flats.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
